name: Tatte Bakery & Cafe
description: The best morning in Boston: a cardamom coffee and a slice of something impossible. The Newbury St location has the best light.

name: Flour Bakery + Cafe
description: Joanne Chang's baking is the standard against which Boston bakeries are judged. The sticky bun is not a metaphor. It is a physical fact.

name: Row 34
description: The best raw bar in the city. Local oysters, serious wine list, no pretension. The burger at lunch is also excellent and people don't talk about it enough.

name: The Beehive
description: Jazz seven nights a week in a basement that feels like Paris in 1955. The cocktails are good. The music is better. Show up early.

name: The Fenway Victory Gardens
description: The oldest continuously operating victory garden in the US. Plots have been growing here since 1942. Walk through it in May.

name: Mike & Patty's
description: Seven-seat breakfast counter on a side street. The fancy egg sandwich will reorganize your morning. Cash only. Worth the wait.

name: Pavement Coffeehouse
description: Before the game, after the game, instead of going to the game. The espresso is consistent in the way that matters.

name: Sportello
description: Lunch counter Italian from Barbara Lynch. Sit at the curved bar, order the pasta, don't rush. Quietly one of the best restaurants in the city.

name: Regina Pizzeria
description: Since 1926. The original location on Thatcher St is the one. Thin crust, brick oven, no delivery. Go there.

name: Trident Booksellers & Cafe
description: Books and coffee in the same room, which is the correct way to spend a Saturday. The back wall is floor-to-ceiling paperbacks.

name: Pho Hoa
description: The real bowl. The broth has been going since before you were born. Fields Corner, not the tourist version.

name: The Sevens Ale House
description: Low ceiling, no fuss, honest pours. One of the last genuine neighborhood bars in Boston. The dart board is not decorative.

name: Arnold Arboretum
description: 247 acres of trees from around the world, free to enter, designed by Olmsted. In May the lilacs bloom and the whole city shows up.

name: Harpoon Brewery
description: The brewery tour ends at the taproom and that's the part that matters. The beer hall has long tables and good pretzels.

name: Brattle Book Shop
description: Three floors of used books, including an outdoor cart with $1 paperbacks. One of the oldest antiquarian bookshops in the country.

name: The Gallows
description: Perfect burger, good beer list, no fuss. The kind of South End restaurant that doesn't need to try hard because it's already good.

name: ICA Boston
description: The building is as good as the collection. Harbor views from the galleries, rotating contemporary shows, free on Thursday evenings.

name: Lamplighter Brewing
description: Cambridge's best taproom. The beer is excellent and the space is big enough to actually sit down. Take the red line.

name: Cafe Luna
description: The Sunday morning coffee place. Regulars, newspapers, no Wi-Fi pressure. Porter Square neighborhood classic.

name: Oleana
description: Ana Sortun's Eastern Mediterranean cooking is the reason to plan a dinner in Cambridge. The mezze spread alone justifies the trip.

name: Castle Island
description: Fort Independence, harbor views, and the best soft serve in the city. Walk the loop, watch the planes land at Logan.
address: 2010 William J Day Blvd Boston MA 02127

name: Santarpio's Pizza
description: Order the pizza. Order the sausages. Don't ask for substitutions. Since 1903 and they don't need to change.

name: McKinney's at Harvard Square
description: The dark bar you want after a long week. Strong drinks, no attitude. On the Cambridge side where things are still honest.

name: South End Buttery
description: Neighborhood brunch without the wait. The French toast is correct. The people watching from the sidewalk tables is even better.

name: Newbury Comics
description: Records, comics, merch. The original Newbury St location is the right one. You will leave with something you didn't plan to buy.

name: Tavolo
description: This is what a neighborhood Italian should be. Affordable, excellent, not trying to impress anyone. Dot Ave is lucky to have it.

name: Area Four
description: Wood-fired pizza at dinner, excellent coffee all day. The morning bun situation is a legitimate reason to visit Cambridge.

name: Coppa
description: Italian enoteca that's been quietly excellent for fifteen years. The charcuterie and the wood-fired dishes. Not flashy, just right.

name: Tasty Burger
description: The burger is $5.50. It is correct. After a day game, this is the call, not the overpriced places on Lansdowne.

name: Market in the Square
description: The Davis Square food hall that locals use. Good cheese, good produce, and a deli counter that doesn't disappoint.

name: Museum of Fine Arts
description: The American wing alone is worth three visits. Free with a Boston Public Library card on Wednesdays. One of the great museums.

name: Notch Brewing
description: Session ales and a taproom that feels like a community space, not a beer hall. The Haus Pils is the best lager in Boston.

name: The Plough and Stars
description: Cambridge's oldest Irish pub. Live music, real pints, honest room. A Thursday night here is better than most Friday nights anywhere.

name: Sofra Bakery
description: Middle Eastern pastries and savory pies for breakfast. The almond croissant situation is excellent. Watertown, technically, but worth it.

name: Constitution Wharf
description: Walk out past the Navy Yard and find the bench with the best view of the harbor in the city. USS Constitution in the background. Free.

name: Haley House Bakery Cafe
description: Social enterprise cafe that's been feeding the neighborhood since 1966. The soups are real, the community is realer.

name: Penguin Pizza
description: Allston's neighborhood pizza. Late night slices, full pies, a room full of people who live nearby. Reliable.

name: Broadsheet Coffee Roasters
description: Downtown's best coffee and one of the few places to get it right in the Financial District. The filter coffee is the move.

name: Blackbird Doughnuts
description: The best doughnuts in Boston and it's not close. The blueberry mascarpone sells out first. Go before 10am.

name: Legal Sea Foods
description: The original. Not the airport version. The clam chowder is legitimately the Boston chowder standard.

name: The Automatic
description: Southie bar that's been here long enough to be real. The crowd is mixed, the beer is cold, and the jukebox is good.

name: Boston Public Library
description: The McKim building on Copley Square is one of the most beautiful rooms in the country. The courtyard alone. Free. Open every day.

name: Secret Garden at Beacon Hill
description: Pinckney Street, halfway up the hill. A courtyard no one tells you about. Sits behind a wrought iron gate that's usually unlocked.

name: O Ya
description: The best sushi in Boston and one of the best in the country. Not casual, not cheap, completely worth it for a special occasion.

name: The Hourly Oyster House
description: A long raw bar and an even longer cocktail menu. The happy hour oysters are legitimately $1 and the room is always buzzing.

name: East Boston Greenway
description: The waterfront path from Maverick to Piers Park. Harbor views, planes landing overhead, miles of walking path. Free and underrated.

name: Notoriety
description: The sneaker and streetwear store that Allston deserves. Good selection, no markup politics, staff who actually know the product.

name: Diesel Cafe
description: Davis Square's original coffeehouse. Pool tables, long hours, the right amount of noise. Open late when you need it.

name: Cunard Tavern
description: The neighborhood bar that Columbia Point forgot to mention. Cheap pints, good wings, a room full of people who actually live here.

name: Boston City Hall Plaza
description: Free outdoor ice rink in winter, farmers market in summer, political rallies all year. The building is brutalist and correct.
